raw:
{
  "criterion": "gherkin",
  "sub_scores": {
    "completeness": 2,
    "alignment": 4,
    "declarative": 3,
    "atomic": 3
  },
  "evidence_quotes": {
    "completeness": [
      "ac-ids.txt lists 29 AC IDs: AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-1.3, AC-1.4, AC-2.1, AC-2.2, AC-2.3, AC-2.4, AC-3.1, AC-3.2, AC-3.3, AC-3.4, AC-3.5, AC-3.6, AC-4.1, AC-4.2, AC-4.3, AC-4.4, AC-4.5, AC-5.1, AC-5.2, AC-5.3, AC-5.4, AC-5.5, AC-5.6, AC-6.1, AC-6.2, AC-6.3, AC-6.4",
      "Feature tags found cover 24/29 unique AC IDs: AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-1.4, AC-2.1, AC-2.2, AC-2.3, AC-2.4, AC-3.1, AC-3.2, AC-3.3, AC-3.4, AC-3.5, AC-4.1, AC-4.2, AC-4.3, AC-4.4, AC-5.1, AC-5.2, AC-5.3, AC-5.4, AC-5.5, AC-6.1, AC-6.2, AC-6.3; missing: AC-1.3, AC-3.6, AC-4.5, AC-5.6, AC-6.4",
      "features/lending.feature: \"When the librarian lends the book to the member\" has no matching step definition in features/step_definitions/steps.ts; many lending, returns, reservations, and fines steps are unwired, capping completeness at 2."
    ],
    "alignment": [
      "features/lending.feature: \"@AC-3.2\" followed by \"Scenario: Reject loan when member has reached loan limit\"",
      "features/returns.feature: \"@AC-4.2\" followed by \"Scenario: Return a book late and create a fine\" and another \"@AC-4.2\" followed by \"Scenario: Return a book very late with fine cap\"",
      "All 26 scenarios carry an @AC tag, and each scenario uses a single AC tag."
    ],
    "declarative": [
      "Good example from features/returns.feature: \"When the librarian records the return before the due date\"",
      "Good example from features/reservations.feature: \"Then the oldest waiting reservation becomes notified\"",
      "Bad example from multiple files: \"Then the system returns a 409 error\"",
      "Bad example from features/fines.feature: \"Then the unpaid fines total is included in the response\""
    ],
    "atomic": [
      "Mostly atomic example from features/returns.feature: \"Then the loan is marked as returned\" followed by \"And the available copies increases by 1\"",
      "Compound assertion from features/catalog.feature: \"And each book shows title, author, ISBN, total copies, and available copies\"",
      "Compound data/action step from features/add_entities.feature: \"When the librarian adds a book with title \\\"Dune\\\", author \\\"Frank Herbert\\\", ISBN \\\"978-0441013593\\\", and 3 copies\""
    ]
  },
  "final_score": 3,
  "confidence": "high"
}

completeness: features/lending.feature: "When the librarian lends the book to the member" has no matching step definition in features/step_definitions/steps.ts; many lending, returns, reservations, and fines steps are unwired, capping completeness at 2.
alignment: features/lending.feature: "@AC-3.2" followed by "Scenario: Reject loan when member has reached loan limit"
features/returns.feature: "@AC-4.2" followed by "Scenario: Return a book late and create a fine" and another "@AC-4.2" followed by "Scenario: Return a book very late with fine cap"
All 26 scenarios carry an @AC tag, and each scenario uses a single AC tag.
declarative: Good example from features/returns.feature: "When the librarian records the return before the due date"
Good example from features/reservations.feature: "Then the oldest waiting reservation becomes notified"
Bad example from multiple files: "Then the system returns a 409 error"
Bad example from features/fines.feature: "Then the unpaid fines total is included in the response"
atomic: Mostly atomic example from features/returns.feature: "Then the loan is marked as returned" followed by "And the available copies increases by 1"
Compound assertion from features/catalog.feature: "And each book shows title, author, ISBN, total copies, and available copies"
Compound data/action step from features/add_entities.feature: "When the librarian adds a book with title \"Dune\", author \"Frank Herbert\", ISBN \"978-0441013593\", and 3 copies"
